requirements.text_summary: Treat the 2025 HLA standard as a coordinated three-document product set: Framework and Rules, Federate Interface Specification, and OMT Specification.
implementation: Cross-link framework rules, federate interface services, and OMT/FOM data requirements.

requirements.text_summary: Do not label an RTI, shim, object model, or tool as HLA Conforming without certified conformance evidence from a recognized/public authority.
implementation: Use surface-backed, lifecycle-green, adapter-smoke-green, core-exchange-green, and trace-green until certified evidence exists.

requirements.text_summary: A federation shall have an HLA FOM documented according to the OMT.
tests: FOM evidence warning/failure for non-trivial demos
implementation: Make Target/Radar the main FOM-backed demo.

requirements.text_summary: Simulation-associated object instance representation shall be in federates, not in the RTI.
tests: RTI state model does not claim semantic object ownership
implementation: RTI may track handles, ownership, subscriptions, queues, and routing state; federates own semantic object state.

requirements.text_summary: During federation execution, all exchange of FOM data among joined federates shall occur via the RTI.
tests: route trace proves backend callback path
implementation: Do not count direct federate-to-federate shortcuts as route proof.

requirements.text_summary: Joined federates shall interact with the RTI according to the HLA interface specification.
tests: HLA-shaped service invocation and callback trace
implementation: Java/C++ shims must prove API-shaped calls, not only byte movement.

requirements.text_summary: During federation execution, an instance attribute shall be owned by at most one joined federate at a time.
implementation: Stage ownership from publisher-owned MVP to negotiated ownership.

requirements.text_summary: Federates shall have a SOM documented according to the OMT.
implementation: Add SOM metadata lane after FOM validation.

requirements.text_summary: Federates shall be able to update/reflect attributes and send/receive interactions as documented.
implementation: Use FOM-backed object/interaction exchange scenarios.

requirements.text_summary: Federates shall be able to transfer and accept ownership dynamically.
implementation: Promote ownership services from staged backlog to compliance scenarios.

requirements.text_summary: Federates shall be able to vary update conditions such as thresholds.
implementation: Track update condition variation as a post-MVP capability.

requirements.text_summary: Federates shall manage local time to coordinate data exchange.
implementation: Keep logical time and callback evidence in route traces.

requirements.text_summary: Service group organization shall drive the capability and test matrix.
implementation: Organize federation, declaration, object, ownership, time, DDM, and support service status separately.

requirements.text_summary: An HLA-conforming RTI shall implement all joined federate-initiated services and invoke all RTI-initiated services defined in the Federate Interface specification.
implementation: Report core scenario status separately from full interface surface and callback status.

requirements.text_summary: RTI conformance requires at least one language-specific API implementation.
implementation: Track Java 2010/2025, C++ 2010/2025, and Python mapping evidence separately.

requirements.text_summary: Java and C++ language bindings matter directly; Python must maintain a documented semantic mapping.
implementation: Keep Java jars and C++ capsules standard-backed while documenting Python mapping evidence.

requirements.text_summary: Illegal operations shall raise RTI exceptions according to the normative state charts.
implementation: Return structured RTI exceptions, not raw Python/Java/C++ crashes.

requirements.text_summary: RTIs shall implement both immediate and evoked callback models.
implementation: Keep evoked callback polling as first proof; report immediate callback evidence as missing until implemented.

requirements.text_summary: The RTI-retained FDD shall include minimum object, interaction, directed interaction, attribute, parameter, dimension, transportation, update rate, and switches tables; names are case-sensitive.
implementation: Parse required FDD tables from 1516.2-2025 XML/DIF.

requirements.text_summary: Create Federation Execution and Join Federation Execution shall reject noncompliant FOM modules with exceptions.
implementation: Reject the whole module rather than partially loading invalid FDD data.

requirements.text_summary: RTI and joined federates shall share a federation-wide logical time implementation; default integer and floating-point logical time implementations are required.
implementation: Keep HLAinteger64Time and HLAfloat64Time factories non-negotiable for 2025.

requirements.text_summary: HLA object model names shall follow XML naming conventions plus HLA-specific restrictions.
implementation: Apply naming validation to object classes, interactions, attributes, parameters, dimensions, transportation types, update rates, data types, switches, and synchronization points.

requirements.text_summary: Object models shall support both OMT tabular format and OMT DIF, with DIF primary and authoritative.
implementation: Prioritize XML/DIF validation over PDF or visual table extraction.

requirements.text_summary: All OMT components shall be completed for FOMs and SOMs, though some tables may be empty or contain only required HLA content.
implementation: Do not fail empty tables unless the standard requires content.

requirements.text_summary: FOMs require MOM/MIM content and the RTI uses MIM first, then specified FOM modules during federation creation.
implementation: Make the loader explicit about MIM and MOM source status.

requirements.text_summary: Object model identification table rows are required, with NA where no information is appropriate.
implementation: Validate Name, Type, Version, Modification date, Security classification, Description, POC, and References.

requirements.text_summary: OMT conformance verification should identify invalid entries explicitly across naming, required identification fields, uniqueness, P/S fields, and table-specific checks.
implementation: Emit structured requirement/table/field/value/status/message validation failures.

requirements.text_summary: FOM/SOM module merge rules are first-class requirements and the RTI shall combine MIM/FOM data according to OMT rules.
implementation: Build a FOM merge engine, not just a parser.
